title: Branching Structures
tags: [branching, structure]
---

# Branching Structures

A branching narrative lives or dies on how its forks reconverge. The
naive approach — every choice spawns a permanently separate path —
explodes combinatorially and starves every branch of wordcount. The
practical alternative is the diamond: a fork that reconverges after a
bounded number of beats, carrying forward a small amount of state
(a flag, a relationship delta) rather than a fully divergent plot.

## Hard forks versus soft forks

Reserve permanently divergent endings for the one or two decisions that
define what kind of story this is. Everything else should reconverge —
the reader's choice should change texture and consequence, not fork the
entire remaining narrative into an unmaintainable tree.

## Residue

When a soft fork reconverges, the road not taken should leave residue:
a remark a character makes, a door that is locked instead of open. Pure
amnesia after convergence is the fastest way to make branching choices
feel decorative.
